papers.title: High Manganese and Aluminum Steels for the Military and Transportation Industry
papers.paper_scope: Review of tensile and high-strain-rate fracture properties of austenitic Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.9C steels, focusing on the effects of silicon (0.59–1.56%) and impurity phosphorus (0.001–0.07%) on age-hardening response, microstructure evolution, and processing-structure-property relationships in cast and wrought conditions.

alloys.alloys_notes: Base composition for reviewed alloys. Fully austenitic when solution treated above 950-C. Density reduction up to 15% compared with quenched and tempered Cr-Mo steels.
Low-silicon, low-phosphorus variant from Table I.
Low-phosphorus, 1.07% Si variant from Table I.
High-silicon, low-phosphorus variant from Table I.
Calcium-treated baseline variant from Table I.
High-phosphorus variant from Table I. Table IV reports this as 0.94% Si, 0.043% P.
High-phosphorus (0.07% P) variant from Table I and Table III.
Hot-rolled lightweight steel shown in Fig. 1b.
High-silicon hot-rolled steel discussed in the notch toughness section.
Benchmark quenched and tempered Cr-Mo steel used for comparison.

processes.description: As-cast condition; cast plates or ingots.
processes.processes_notes: Cast steels have low melting points, less than 1350-C, and excellent filling characteristics.

processes.description: Solution treated at 1050-C for 2 h and water quenched.
processes.processes_notes: Produces fully austenitic matrix. Slow quenching can cause heterogeneous precipitation of k-carbide along grain boundaries.

processes.description: Solution treated at 900-C for 10 min and water quenched.
processes.processes_notes: Used for wrought hot-rolled material to produce equiaxed grain structure.

processes.description: Solution treated at 1100-C for 10 min and water quenched.
processes.processes_notes: Applied to hot-rolled high-silicon steel to improve toughness.
Primary age-hardening temperature. Precipitation of nanosized k-carbide. Times range from 10 h to 90 h depending on alloy.
Higher aging temperature used for some calcium-treated steels.

processes.description: Hot rolled at 900-C to an 84% reduction in thickness, then solution treated at 900-C for 10 min and water quenched.
processes.processes_notes: Wrought processing producing equiaxed austenite grains 10 lm to 20 lm in diameter.

processes.description: Hot rolled only, slow cooled after deformation.
processes.processes_notes: Results in SRO and precipitation of k-carbide due to slow cooling, leading to brittle behavior.
Benchmark heat treatment for SAE 4130 cast steel.

processing_steps.processing_steps_notes: Solution treated to produce fully austenitic matrix.
Hot rolled to produce fine equiaxed austenite grains with annealing twins.
Hot rolled and slow cooled after deformation.
Solution treatment after hot rolling to dissolve k-carbide and improve toughness.
Hot rolled at 900-C to 84% reduction in thickness.
Solution treated to produce equiaxed grains 10-20 lm in diameter.
Peak aged condition. Precipitation of k-carbide on grain boundaries.

structures.overall_structure: High-phosphorus aged microstructure with larger ordered domains

precipitates.distribution: high number density, coarsening along elastically soft <100>

interfaces.phase_evolution: Precipitation of k-carbide in the temperature range 450-700-C results from compositional modulation by spinodal decomposition. Silicon increases carbon partitioning coefficient from 2.1 to 2.9, increasing constrained mismatch and hardening.

interface_notes.stress_strain_distribution: Phosphorus substitution for aluminum in k-carbide relaxes local bonding along <100>, resulting in a 45% reduction in cleavage stress of k-carbide.

interfaces.phase_evolution: Phosphorus increases the prevalence of ordering reaction and accelerates spinodal decomposition during aging. P substitutes for Al corner positions in k-carbide, predicted to create open-volume defects on {100} under stress.

comparative_performance.benchmark_alloy: Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.9C with 1.07% Si aged to 310 BHN

characterization_methods.purpose: Microstructural analysis of k-carbide precipitation and crystal structure
characterization_methods.key_findings: Confirmed nanosized k-carbide with E21 cubic perovskite structure and cube-on-cube orientation relationship with austenite.

characterization_methods.purpose: Chemical analysis of carbon partitioning and compositional modulation
characterization_methods.key_findings: Carbon partitioning coefficient in k-carbide increased from 2.1 to 2.9 with increasing Si from 0.59% to 1.56% after aging 60 h at 530-C.

characterization_methods.purpose: Phase identification and lattice parameter measurement
characterization_methods.key_findings: No spinodal decomposition or SRO in solution-treated specimens regardless of Si content. Silicon decreases austenite lattice parameter during aging.

characterization_methods.purpose: Microstructural imaging of as-cast and hot-rolled microstructures
characterization_methods.key_findings: As-cast alloys consist of large primary austenitic dendrites producing millimeter-scale grain structures.
Hot-rolled steel consists of fine equiaxed austenite grains and annealing twins, average grain size 50 um, pinned by NbC on grain boundaries.

characterization_methods.technique: SEM with ASPEX automated feature analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: High AlN inclusion density is deleterious to impact properties. Porosity in 0.59% Si steel was more than twice that of 1.07% Si steel.

computational_details.validation_against_experiment: Experimental APT and XRD results confirm predictions

analytical_model.parameters: 120 MPa/wt.% Si for specimens aged at 530-C for 60 h
analytical_model.model_note: Derived from the work of Kato et al. Predicted increase in yield strength due to Si addition is in reasonable agreement with reported mechanical properties.

computational_details.validation_against_experiment: Fracture mode observations support predicted 45% reduction in cleavage stress

analytical_model.model_note: Substitution of P for Al positions in k-carbide is most favorable. Predicted to create open-volume defects on {100} under stress, relaxing local bonding along <100> and reducing cleavage stress by 45%.

analytical_model.model_name: SRO kinetics of P and Al
analytical_model.parameters: SRO of P into E21 corner positions may be an order of magnitude faster than Al
analytical_model.model_note: Thermodynamic preference of P substitution for Al was not taken into account.

unmapped_findings: Figure 2a (scatter plot embedded in text) gives 530-C age-hardening hardness values for low-P Fe-30Mn-9Al-XSi-0.9C steels: at 1 h, 0.59% Si = 210 BHN, 1.07% Si = 220 BHN, 1.56% Si = 215 BHN; at 10 h, 260, 280, 295 BHN; at 100 h, 340, 350, 345 BHN.

Figure 2b (scatter plot embedded in text) gives 530-C age-hardening hardness values for 1% Si steels with varying P: at 1 h, 0.001% P = 215 BHN, 0.006% P = 225 BHN, 0.018% P = 275 BHN, 0.043% P = 245 BHN; at 10 h, 260, 275, 360, 340 BHN; at 100 h, 325, 345, 380, 370 BHN.

Table II contains additional tensile data for non-calcium-treated steels with higher P (0.06% P): 1.0Si ST UTS 687 MPa EL 44%; 1.4Si ST UTS 766 MPa EL 36%; 1.0Si aged 530-C 10h UTS 940 MPa EL 18%; 1.4Si aged 10h UTS 995 MPa EL 14%; 1.0Si aged 30h UTS 1065 MPa EL 8%; 1.4Si aged 30h UTS 1085 MPa EL 5%. A calcium-treated 0.78Si 0.007P as-cast alloy had YS 616 MPa, UTS 698 MPa, EL 17%.

Figure 7 scatter data indicate wrought Fe-30Mn-9Al-1.01Si-0.9C-0.5Mo UTS values of approximately 950 MPa (as-solution treated), 1050 MPa (2h aged), 1070 MPa (10h), and 1160 MPa (50h), with corresponding cast values of 760 MPa (20h) and 1000 MPa (50h). Wrought elongation ranged from about 15% at 1160 MPa UTS to 55% at 950 MPa UTS, while cast elongation was about 18% at 1000 MPa and 36% at 770 MPa.

Figure 11 line/scatter data for CVN versus temperature: 1.07% Si 12h aged at 530-C gives energies from 42 J at -40-C to 92 J at 25-C; 1.56% Si 12h aged gives 21 J at -40-C to 68 J at 25-C; 1.07% Si 30h aged gives 20 J at -40-C to 58 J at 25-C; 1.56% Si 30h aged gives 12 J at -40-C to 33 J at 25-C.

Hot-rolled Fe-30Mn-9Al-2.7Si-0.9C with 0.023% P had CVN breaking energy of only 23 J (L-T) and 22 J (T-L) in the as-hot-rolled condition, improving to 160 J (L-T) after solution treatment for 10 min at 1100-C and water quenching.

The density reduction of these lightweight steels is up to 15% compared with conventional quenched and tempered steels and 17% reduction potential is mentioned for Al contents between 4 and 12 weight percent.

Inclusion analysis (Fig. 15) shows total inclusion density similar between low-P Si-containing steels (62-78 inclusions/mm2), with the major difference being porosity: 0.59% Si steel had more than twice the porosity of 1.07% Si steel and almost double that of 1.56% Si steel.
Solution-treated cast steels with low phosphorus exhibit room-temperature Charpy V-notch impact energies up to 200 J and dynamic fracture toughness over 700 kJ/m2.

Phosphorus in levels greater than 0.06% P greatly increases the kinetics of age hardening but sharply decreases CVN impact toughness by as much as 80%. AlN inclusions reduced CVN breaking energy at -40-C by almost 50%.
